---
title: 'Define: Oil'
date: '2015-08-08T16:58:00.0020000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Discerning The Time'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '5516348699572841887'
blogger_filename: '/2015/08/define-oil.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Discerning The Time/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Definitions'
---

<br />
<h3>
There are over 200 search results in the NIV translation for "Oil". I think God is trying to tell us something.</h3>
<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/search/?q=oil&amp;c=&amp;t=niv&amp;ps=100&amp;s=Bibles" target="_blank">http://www.biblestudytools.com/search/?q=oil&amp;c=&amp;t=niv&amp;ps=100&amp;s=Bibles</a> <br />
<br />
<h4>
Other Resources:</h4>
<blockquote class="tr_bq">
<a href="http://www.ancient-hebrew.org/emagazine/049.pdf" target="_blank">Biblical Word of the Month - Oil</a><a href="http://www.ancient-hebrew.org/emagazine/049.pdf" target="_blank"></a><br />
By: Jeff A. Benner<br />
In todayâ€™s culture, oil symbolizes wealth. Nothing has changed over thousands of years.<br />
In the ancient cutlures of the near and middle east, oil was a symbol of wealth. Oil was<br />
used in lamps for making light. Different types of oils, such as the olive oil, were used for medicinal purposes. Oil was poured on one who was taking the office of king or priest. Oil was used in offerings to Elohiym. The Hebrew word for oil is ×©×ž×Ÿ (shemen, Strong's #8081 and shamen, Strong's #8082). The verb form is ×©×ž×Ÿ (shaman, Strong's #8080) and means â€œto be fat.â€ The parent root of this word is ×©× (shem, Strong's #8034), which is usually translated as â€œnameâ€ but more Hebraicly means â€œcharacter.â€ Isnâ€™t your character your wealth? Isnâ€™t the character of YHWH his wealth?</blockquote>
<br />
<blockquote class="tr_bq">
<a href="http://www.ancient-hebrew.org/emagazine/011.html" target="_blank">Biblical Word of the Month - Messiah</a><a href="http://www.ancient-hebrew.org/emagazine/011.html" target="_blank"></a><br />
<div class="MsoNormal">
By: Jeff A. Benner</div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
The Hebrew word <span dir="rtl" lang="HE" style="mso-ascii-font-family: OLBHEB; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-hansi-font-family: OLBHEB;">×ž×©×™×—</span><span dir="ltr"></span><span dir="ltr"></span><span lang="HE"><span dir="ltr"></span><span dir="ltr"></span> </span>(mashiach/mah-shee-ahch - where the "ch" is pronounced hard as in the name Bach - Strongs #4899) is usually transliterated as Messiah. Let us first examine how this word is transliterated and translated. In the Tenach/Old Testament this word is usually translated in the English as "Anointed One" and occasionally transliterated as "Messiah". In the Greek Septuagint (LXX) this Hebrew word is translated with the Greek word "christos" and is transliterated as "Christ". </div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br />
The root word of meshiyach is the verb mashach (Strong's #4886) meaning "to anoint". In the ancient Hebrew culture is customary to pour oil on the head of one who is being given a position of authority. This practice is called "anointing". One of the most common misunderstandings about meshiyach is that there is only one, but the Tenach identifies several. The word is used 39 times and just a few of these are listed below.</div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br />
Leviticus 4:3 If the anointed (mashiyach) priest (kohen) sins bringing guilt to the people....<br />
<br />
1 Samuel 24:6 And he (David) said to his men, YHWH forbid me if I should do this thing to my lord (Saul) the anointed (mashiyach) of YHWH</div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br />
1 Chronicles 16:22 Do not touch my anointed (mashiyach), my prophets do not harm.</div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br />
I chose these three passages for one reason, it demonstrates, from an Hebraic perspective, who are mashiyach. The Priests, Kings and Prophets of <st1:country-region w:st="on">Israel</st1:country-region> are the mashiyach of <st1:place w:st="on"><st1:country-region w:st="on">Israel</st1:country-region></st1:place>, they are the ones who are anointed as men of authority.</div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br />
While the original meaning of the word mashiyach is applied to one who is actually anointed with oil, it by extension can also refer to anyone who holds an office of authority whether they were anointed or not. The Tenach identifies Cyrus, the King of Persia as a mashiyach.</div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br />
Isaiah 45:1 Thus says YHWH to his anointed (mashiyach) Cyrus (the King of Persia)...</div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br />
Without going into the controversy about who the coming mashiyach (Messiah) was, is or will be, our purpose here is to understand the Hebraic concept of who and what a mashiyach is. This will give us a foundation within or our own studies about the mashiyach.</div>
</blockquote>
<br />
<blockquote class="tr_bq">
<a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Holy_anointing_oil" target="_blank">WIKI</a><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Holy_anointing_oil" target="_blank"></a><br />
<br />
The <b>holy anointing oil</b> (Hebrew: <i>shemen</i> "oil", <i>ha-mishchah</i> "of anointing" ×©×ž×Ÿ ×”×ž×©×—×”) formed an integral part of the ordination of the&nbsp;<a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Priesthood_(Ancient_Israel)" title="Priesthood (Ancient Israel)">priesthood</a><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Holy_anointing_oil" target="_blank"> and the </a><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/High_Priest_(Judaism)" title="High Priest (Judaism)">high priest</a><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Holy_anointing_oil" target="_blank"> as well as in the consecration of the articles of the </a><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tabernacle" title="Tabernacle">tabernacle</a><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Holy_anointing_oil" target="_blank"> (Exodus 30:26) and subsequent </a><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Temple_in_Jerusalem" title="Temple in Jerusalem">temples in Jerusalem</a><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Holy_anointing_oil" target="_blank">. The primary purpose of </a><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anointing" title="Anointing">anointing</a><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Holy_anointing_oil" target="_blank"> with the holy anointing oil was to cause the anointed persons or objects to become <i>qodesh</i> â€“ most holy (Exodus 30:29).</a>Originally the oil was used exclusively for the priests and the Tabernacle articles but was later extended to include prophets and kings (I Samuel 10:1). It was forbidden to be used on an outsider (Exodus 30:33) or to be used on the body of any common persons (Ex. 30:32a) and the&nbsp;<a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Israelites" title="Israelites">Israelites</a><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Holy_anointing_oil" target="_blank"> were forbidden to duplicate any like it for themselves (Ex. 30:32b).</a></blockquote>
<br />
<blockquote class="tr_bq">
<a href="http://www.jewishencyclopedia.com/articles/1559-anointing" target="_blank">Anointing Jewish Encyclopedia</a><a href="http://www.jewishencyclopedia.com/articles/1559-anointing" target="_blank"></a><br />
<br />
Two words are employed in the Old Testament for Anointing, <img src="/images/imported/d3sva65x0i5hnc-cloudfront-net/v01p611003-1a86d090e634.jpg" /> and <img src="/images/imported/d3sva65x0i5hnc-cloudfront-net/v01p611004-aced24746ca4.jpg" />. The former designates the private use of unguents in making one's toilet, the latter their use as a religious rite.</blockquote>
<br />
<h4>
Anoint The Preist:</h4>
<h4 class="ResultTitle">
<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/leviticus/8-30.html">Leviticus 8:30 NIV</a></h4>
<div class="ResultText">
<blockquote class="tr_bq">
<div class="ResultText">
Then Moses took some of the anointing <span class="Highlight">oil</span> and some of the blood from the altar and sprinkled them on Aaron and his garments and on his sons and their garments. So he consecrated Aaron and his garments and his sons and their garments.</div>
</blockquote>
<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/exodus/passage.aspx?q=exodus+29:2-12" target="_blank">Exodus 29:2-12</a></div>
<blockquote class="tr_bq">
4 Then bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance to the Tent of Meeting and wash them with water. 5 Take the garments and dress Aaron with the tunic, the robe of the ephod, the ephod itself and the breastpiece. Fasten the ephod on him by its skillfully woven waistband. 6 Put the turban on his head and attach the sacred diadem to the turban. 7 Take the anointing oil and anoint him by pouring it on his head.</blockquote>
<h4>
<br />Making the Anointing Oil:</h4>
<br />
<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/exodus/passage.aspx?q=exodus+30:19-29" target="_blank">Exodus 30:19-29</a>&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/exodus/passage.aspx?q=exodus+30:22-33">Anointing Oil</a>:<br />
<span class="versetext" id="ex30-22" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum"></span></span><br />
<blockquote class="tr_bq">
<span class="versetext" id="ex30-22" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum"><span class="versetext" id="ex30-22" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">22</span> Then the LORD said to Moses, </span><span class="versetext" id="ex30-23" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">23</span> "Take the following fine spices:<a href="https://draft.blogger.com/null" name="6"></a><sup class="crossref" style="display: none;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/exodus/passage.aspx?q=exodus+30:19-29#cr-descriptionAnchor-6" id="6" jquery18105771473353641618="72" title="S Ge 43:11"></a></sup> 500 shekels<a href="https://draft.blogger.com/null" name="a"></a><sup class="footnote" style="display: none;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/exodus/passage.aspx?q=exodus+30:19-29#fn-descriptionAnchor-a" id="a" jquery18105771473353641618="65" title="That is, about 12 1/2 pounds (about 6 kilograms)"></a></sup> of liquid myrrh,<a href="https://draft.blogger.com/null" name="7"></a><sup class="crossref" style="display: none;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/exodus/passage.aspx?q=exodus+30:19-29#cr-descriptionAnchor-7" id="7" jquery18105771473353641618="73" title="S Ge 37:25"></a></sup> half as much (that is, 250 shekels) of fragrant cinnamon,<a href="https://draft.blogger.com/null" name="8"></a><sup class="crossref" style="display: none;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/exodus/passage.aspx?q=exodus+30:19-29#cr-descriptionAnchor-8" id="8" jquery18105771473353641618="74" title="Pr 7:17; SS 4:14"></a></sup> 250 shekels of fragrant cane,<a href="https://draft.blogger.com/null" name="9"></a><sup class="crossref" style="display: none;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/exodus/passage.aspx?q=exodus+30:19-29#cr-descriptionAnchor-9" id="9" jquery18105771473353641618="75" title="SS 4:14; Isa 43:24; Jer 6:20"></a></sup> </span><span class="versetext highlightThenFade" id="ex30-24"><span class="versenum">24</span> 500 shekels<a class="highlightThenFade" href="https://draft.blogger.com/null" name="10"></a><sup class="crossref" style="display: none;"><a class="highlightThenFade" href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/exodus/passage.aspx?q=exodus+30:19-29#cr-descriptionAnchor-10" id="10" jquery18105771473353641618="76" title="S ver 13"></a></sup> of cassia<a class="highlightThenFade" href="https://draft.blogger.com/null" name="11"></a><sup class="crossref" style="display: none;"><a class="highlightThenFade" href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/exodus/passage.aspx?q=exodus+30:19-29#cr-descriptionAnchor-11" id="11" jquery18105771473353641618="77" title="Ps 45:8; Eze 27:19"></a></sup>--all according to the sanctuary shekel--and a hin<a class="highlightThenFade" href="https://draft.blogger.com/null" name="b"></a><sup class="footnote" style="display: none;"><a class="highlightThenFade" href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/exodus/passage.aspx?q=exodus+30:19-29#fn-descriptionAnchor-b" id="b" jquery18105771473353641618="66" title="That is, probably about 4 quarts (about 4 liters)"></a></sup> of olive oil. </span><span class="versetext" id="ex30-25" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">25</span> Make these into a sacred anointing oil, a fragrant blend, the work of a perfumer.<a href="https://draft.blogger.com/null" name="12"></a><sup class="crossref" style="display: none;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/exodus/passage.aspx?q=exodus+30:19-29#cr-descriptionAnchor-12" id="12" jquery18105771473353641618="78" title="ver 35; Ex 37:29; 1Ch 9:30"></a></sup> It will be the sacred anointing oil.<a href="https://draft.blogger.com/null" name="13"></a><sup class="crossref" style="display: none;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/exodus/passage.aspx?q=exodus+30:19-29#cr-descriptionAnchor-13" id="13" jquery18105771473353641618="79" title="S Ex 29:7; S 1Sa 9:16; Ex 40:9"></a></sup> </span><span class="versetext" id="ex30-26" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">26</span> Then use it to anoint<a href="https://draft.blogger.com/null" name="14"></a><sup class="crossref" style="display: none;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/exodus/passage.aspx?q=exodus+30:19-29#cr-descriptionAnchor-14" id="14" jquery18105771473353641618="80" title="Ex 40:9; Lev 8:10; Nu 7:1"></a></sup> the Tent of Meeting, the ark of the Testimony, </span><span class="versetext" id="ex30-27" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">27</span> the table and all its articles, the lampstand and its accessories, the altar of incense, </span><span class="versetext" id="ex30-28" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">28</span> the altar of burnt offering and all its utensils, and the basin with its stand. </span><span class="versetext" id="ex30-29" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">29</span> You shall consecrate them<a href="https://draft.blogger.com/null" name="15"></a><sup class="crossref" style="display: none;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/exodus/passage.aspx?q=exodus+30:19-29#cr-descriptionAnchor-15" id="15" jquery18105771473353641618="81" title="Lev 8:10-11"></a></sup> so they will be most holy, and whatever touches them will be holy.<a href="https://draft.blogger.com/null" name="16"></a><sup class="crossref" style="display: none;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/exodus/passage.aspx?q=exodus+30:19-29#cr-descriptionAnchor-16" id="16" jquery18105771473353641618="82" title="Ex 29:37; Lev 6:18,27; Mt 23:17"></a></sup> </span></span></span></blockquote>
<span class="versetext" id="ex30-22" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">
</span></span><br />
<h4>
Anoint The King</h4>
<br />
<blockquote class="tr_bq">
<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/1-samuel/10-1.html">1 Samuel 10:1 NIV</a>Then Samuel took a flask of <span class="Highlight">oil</span> and poured it on Saul's head and kissed him, saying, "Has not the LORD anointed you leader over his inheritance?<br />
<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/1-samuel/16-1.html">1 Samuel 16:1 NIV</a><br />
[Samuel Anoints David] The LORD said to Samuel, "How long will you mourn for Saul, since I have rejected him as king over Israel? Fill your horn with <span class="Highlight">oil</span> and be on your way; I am sending you to Jesse of Bethlehem. I have chosen one of his sons to be king."<br />
<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/psalms/45-7.html">Psalm 45:7 NIV</a>You love righteousness and hate wickedness; therefore God, your God, has set you above your companions by anointing you with the <span class="Highlight">oil</span> of joy.<br />
<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/hebrews/1-9.html">Hebrews 1:9 NIV</a>You have loved righteousness and hated wickedness; therefore God, your God, has set you above your companions by anointing you with the <span class="Highlight">oil</span> of joy."</blockquote>
<br />
<h4>
Joy. Blessing.</h4>
<br />
<blockquote class="tr_bq">
<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/isaiah/61-3.html">Isaiah 61:3 NIV</a>and provide for those who grieve in Zion-- to bestow on them a crown of beauty instead of ashes, the <span class="Highlight">oil</span> of gladness instead of mourning, and a garment of praise instead of a spirit of despair. They will be called oaks of righteousness, a planting of the LORD for the display of his splendor.</blockquote>
<br />
<blockquote class="tr_bq">
<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/psalms/23-5.html">Psalm 23:5 NIV</a>You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies. You anoint my head with <span class="Highlight">oil</span>; my cup overflows.</blockquote>
<br />
<h4>
Healing.</h4>
<br />
<blockquote class="tr_bq">
<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/james/5-14.html">James 5:14 NIV</a>Is any one of you sick? He should call the elders of the church to pray over him and anoint him with <span class="Highlight">oil</span> in the name of the Lord.</blockquote>
<br />
<h4>
Other.</h4>
<blockquote class="tr_bq">
<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/ezekiel/16-19.html">Ezekiel 16:19 NIV</a>Also the food I provided for you--the fine flour, olive <span class="Highlight">oil</span> and honey I gave you to eat--you offered as fragrant incense before them. That is what happened, declares the Sovereign LORD.<br />
<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/ezekiel/23-41.html">Ezekiel 23:41 NIV</a>You sat on an elegant couch, with a table spread before it on which you had placed the incense and <span class="Highlight">oil</span> that belonged to me.<br />
<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/matthew/25-3.html">Matthew 25:3 NIV</a>The foolish ones took their lamps but did not take any <span class="Highlight">oil</span> with them.<br />
<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/matthew/25-4.html">Matthew 25:4 NIV</a>The wise, however, took <span class="Highlight">oil</span> in jars along with their lamps.<br />
<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/matthew/25-9.html">Matthew 25:9 NIV</a>" 'No,' they replied, 'there may not be enough for both us and you. Instead, go to those who sell <span class="Highlight">oil</span> and buy some for yourselves.'<br />
<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/matthew/25-10.html">Matthew 25:10 NIV</a>"But while they were on their way to buy the <span class="Highlight">oil</span>, the bridegroom arrived. The virgins who were ready went in with him to the wedding banquet. And the door was shut.<br />
<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/luke/10-34.html">Luke 10:34 NIV</a>He went to him and bandaged his wounds, pouring on <span class="Highlight">oil</span> and wine. Then he put the man on his own donkey, took him to an inn and took care of him.</blockquote>
<br />
<br />
<br />
Thanks for studying with me.<br />
<br />
By&nbsp;<a href="https://plus.google.com/+DarrellWolfe/posts" target="_blank">DarrellWolfe</a><br />
<br />
<a href="http://discerningthetime.blogspot.com/" target="_blank">DiscerningTheTime</a><br />
<br />
<a href="http://discerningthetime.blogspot.com/" target="_blank">http://discerningthetime.blogspot.com</a><br />
<br />
<br />
